html:
Exclusive to:

InactiveActiveChargingDestabilizingOn cooldown

The Ancient Gateway is an indestructible Structure exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It is located in the middle of the Atrium Biome.

Its only use is to have an Ancient Key be placed in its socket, making the Ruin's Nightmare Cycle be locked at the peak state. It can be removed again, unlocking the Cycle from its peak. If a fully-constructed Odd Skeleton is revived near an activated Ancient Gateway, the fight against the Ancient Fuelweaver will begin. The Ancient Fuelweaver can deactivate the Atrium's Nightmare Cycle by removing the Ancient Key. If the Ancient Fuelweaver is killed it will trigger an explosion that will activate after three minutes, renewing the Ruins. Twenty days later, the Gateway will accept the Ancient Key to be placed again.

After the Ancient Fuelweaver is killed, a Beckoning Hand will spawn, prompting the player to give it 5 Dreadstones. When given the Dreadstones, Charlie will appear and fix the Ancient Gateway, activating the Shadow Rift cycle.

After fixing the Ancient Gateway, another Beckoning Hand will spawn and ask for Keystone. Giving Keystone will reset the Sanctum.

In a lore sense, the Ancient Gateway acts as a portal to what are known as Gate Realms. These Gate Realms were the settings for special limited-time Events. The repaired gate can be spawned via the console with the following command: c_spawn("atrium_gate").AnimState:AddOverrideBuild("atrium_gate_build")

The Forge

Only during the Event: The Forge

ActiveInactive

In The Forge event, the players spawn in a plume of flames via the Molten Gateway into the Forge Gate Realm. Players must win against the minions of Battlemaster Pugna in order to activate the Gateway with the Ancient Key and leave this Gate Realm. The Molten Gateway is technically a separate structure sharing the same name as the one located in the Atrium. It currently has no other function, but the game files reveal that it also has an active state, which might be used in later versions of the event.

The Gorge

Only during the Event: The Gorge

In The Gorge event, the players spawn in a gush of water via the Mossy Gateway into the Gorge Gate Realm. Chronologically, this event takes place directly after The Forge, meaning that entering the Molten Gateway took the characters straight to this dimension. Players must cook food to satisfy the Gnaw and gain three of the Gnaw's Favors, which are used to charge the Ancient Key and thus open the Mossy Gateway. The Mossy Gateway is technically a separate structure sharing the same name as the one located in the Atrium.

Trivia

  • The Ancient Gateway was introduced in the Heart of the Ruins update.
  • When first released, the Ancient Gateway had black and yellow caution tape all over it, to signalize it was still a work in progress feature. During this time, various Items were able to be placed in, such as the Iridescent Gem. Picking it back out always produced an Ancient Key. When examining an Ancient Gateway with a key inserted, the player character said: "The lights reveal ancient text: Come Back Next Update."
  • The Gateway is featured in the What Lies Beyond? video clip, for a brief moment revealing a silhouette of Winona before her official reveal.
  • The Ancient Gateway located in the Ruins area of the Constant is also called the Atrium Gate.
  • WX-78's examination quote for an inactive Ancient Gateway is "ERROR 502". Error 502 is an error that occurs when a server receives an invalid response from another server due to a "bad gateway", thus being used to refer to the non-working state of this structure.
  • Ancient Gateway is automatically repaired if Shadow Rifts is set to “Always” in world settings.

Gallery

  • The Gateway exploding.
  • After the explosions.
  • The Gateway after the Ancient Fuelweaver dies.
  • A hidden image of the Ancient Gateway alongside the Ancient Fuelweaver in the A New Reign trailer.
  • The Ancient Gateway, as seen in the What Lies Beyond? video clip.
  • The Ancient Gateway, as seen in The Forge cinematic.
  • Charlie at the Ancient Gateway, as seen in The Forge cinematic.
  • The work in progress visual of the Ancient Gateway.
  • The Ancient Gateway's textures as found in the game files when it was first released.
  • The Mossy Gateway, as seen in The Gorge cinematic.
  • Concept art of the Forge version of the Gateway from Rhymes with Play # 167.
  • Concept art of the Gorge version of the Gateway from Rhymes with Play # 201.
  • Concept art of the Gorge version of the Gateway from Rhymes with Play # 201.
  • The Ancient Gateway in a promotional image from Rhymes with Play #208.
  • The Ancient Gateway as seen in a drawing from Art Stream # 41.
StructuresThe Forge ContentThe Gorge Content

Ancient Gateway
Normal
Active
Object Info
Perk
Locks the Ruins into the Nightmare Phase while the Ancient Key is inserted.
20 days (after activating)
Source
Atrium
Miscellaneous
"atrium_gate"
Exclusive to:
InactiveActiveChargingDestabilizingOn cooldown
“The essential components are still intact.”
–Wilson
“Why would anyone want to live down here?”
–Willow
“Is very old thing.”
–Wolfgang
“A beacon of hope left unlit... How fitting.”
–Wendy
“ERROR 502”
–WX-78
“Now where might that lead?”
–Wickerbottom
“Where d'you think it goes, Lucy?”
–Woodie
“It lacks a key.”
–Maxwell
“Tis the Bifröst!”
–Wigfrid
“We need another piece to turn it on.”
–Webber
“All the pieces are back in place.”
–Warly
“Needs fixing”
–Wormwood
“Oughta turn on somehow.”
–Winona
“The thing I hopped through!”
–Wortox
“Someone broke it.”
–Wurt
“It looks like it's missing a piece.”
–Walter
“With a bit of tinkering, I could get it working again.”
–Wanda
“Back in working order.”
–Wilson
“It lit up!”
–Willow
“Little lights is on now.”
–Wolfgang
“Hope springs infernal.”
–Wendy
“POWERED ON”
–WX-78
“That's taken care of.”
–Wickerbottom
“That oughta do it.”
–Woodie
“This is a most wretched idea.”
–Maxwell
“A light tö guide the way!”
–Wigfrid
“Does this mean we get to go home?”
–Webber
“It's ready.”
–Warly
“Working now!”
–Wormwood
“Not sure I trust strange portals anymore.”
–Winona
“That's how you get imps. Hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Shouldn't have done that!”
–Wurt
“It's working!”
–Walter
“It's radiating a strange energy, almost like...”
–Wanda
“It's gaining power.”
–Wilson, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“Something weird's going on.”
–Willow, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“Looks very scary now!”
–Wolfgang, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“Take us far from here.”
–Wendy, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“GATEWAY CHARGING”
–WX-78, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“It is storing power.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“It's soaking up energy, hey?”
–Woodie, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“It is feeding off the dark energy.”
–Maxwell, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“Söon it will öpen the path!”
–Wigfrid, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“What's it doing?”
–Webber, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“I think it's charging up.”
–Warly, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“Oh. What's it doing?”
–Wormwood, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“It's gearing up for something.”
–Winona, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“Oh, we're going to have some fun!”
–Wortox, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“What happening?!”
–Wurt, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“I guess we'll have to wait for it to power up.”
–Walter, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“Does anyone else feel a crackle of magic in the air?”
–Wanda, when examining the Ancient Gateway while fighting the Ancient Fuelweaver
“The gateway is destabilizing.”
–Wilson
“Is it gonna explode?!”
–Willow
“Is going to blow!”
–Wolfgang
“Oh well. I hope it's a quick death.”
–Wendy
“IT WAS A DEATHTRAP ALL ALONG”
–WX-78
“We'd best not be around when it goes off.”
–Wickerbottom
“That'd be my cue to leave.”
–Woodie
“Now we've gone and done it.”
–Maxwell
“Trickery! The Bifröst was öf Löki!”
–Wigfrid
“It looks like it's gonna blow up!”
–Webber
“Something unsafe is happening.”
–Warly
“Looks sick”
–Wormwood
“I don't think we oughta be here when it goes off!”
–Winona
“Hyuyu, it's getting weird!”
–Wortox
“G-glorp!”
–Wurt
“Is it supposed to be making that sound?”
–Walter
“The magical field is collapsing!”
–Wanda
“It needs time to recover. Me too.”
–Wilson
“I'll come back another time.”
–Willow
“Is needing to recharge.”
–Wolfgang
“We can't do that again for awhile.”
–Wendy
“COOLDOWN IN PROGRESS”
–WX-78
“We must be patient.”
–Wickerbottom
“Don't wanna overdo it.”
–Woodie
“Best not to overuse this power.”
–Maxwell
“It shan't wörk again för söme time.”
–Wigfrid
“It wasn't a way home, anyway.”
–Webber
“It's in a recovery period.”
–Warly
“Tired?”
–Wormwood
“It won't be running again for awhile.”
–Winona
“Playtime's over.”
–Wortox
“It over now?”
–Wurt
“I guess I should give it a rest for now.”
–Walter
“For the time being, I think it should be left alone.”
–Wanda

The Ancient Gateway is an indestructible Structure exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It is located in the middle of the Atrium Biome.
Its only use is to have an Ancient Key be placed in its socket, making the Ruin's Nightmare Cycle be locked at the peak state. It can be removed again, unlocking the Cycle from its peak. If a fully-constructed Odd Skeleton is revived near an activated Ancient Gateway, the fight against the Ancient Fuelweaver will begin. The Ancient Fuelweaver can deactivate the Atrium's Nightmare Cycle by removing the Ancient Key. If the Ancient Fuelweaver is killed it will trigger an explosion that will activate after three minutes, renewing the Ruins. Twenty days later, the Gateway will accept the Ancient Key to be placed again.
After the Ancient Fuelweaver is killed, a Beckoning Hand will spawn, prompting the player to give it 5 Dreadstones. When given the Dreadstones, Charlie will appear and fix the Ancient Gateway, activating the Shadow Rift cycle.
After fixing the Ancient Gateway, another Beckoning Hand will spawn and ask for Keystone. Giving Keystone will reset the Sanctum.
In a lore sense, the Ancient Gateway acts as a portal to what are known as Gate Realms. These Gate Realms were the settings for special limited-time Events.
The repaired gate can be spawned via the console with the following command:
c_spawn("atrium_gate").AnimState:AddOverrideBuild("atrium_gate_build")
The Forge[edit | edit source]
Ancient Gateway
Source
The Forge
Miscellaneous
"lavaarena_portal"
Only during the Event: The Forge
ActiveInactive
“I'll just be going now.”
–Wilson
“Time to go.”
–Willow
“Goodbye, puny fire world!”
–Wolfgang
“I bid you good day.”
–Wendy
“READY FOR TRANSPORT”
–WX-78
“Well, this was an unexpected foray.”
–Wickerbottom
“Let's head back to camp, Lucy.”
–Woodie
“Let us end this chapter of our journey.”
–Maxwell
“We dive, back intö the ether!”
–Wigfrid
“Time to go... "home".”
–Webber
“Let's go, tout suite!”
–Warly
“Can go now”
–Wormwood
“Welp, time to go!”
–Winona
“Hop, skip and a jump! Hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Bye-bye!”
–Wurt
“I'll just be going now.”
–Walter
“I'll just be going now.”
–Wanda
“That's how we got here. Hopefully how we get back, too.”
–Wilson
“This is WAY better than our gate back home!”
–Willow
“Is how Wolfgang got here.”
–Wolfgang
“I dared not hope it would take me home.”
–Wendy
“IT IS INACTIVE”
–WX-78
“It is linked to our Gateway, but inactive on this side.”
–Wickerbottom
“Don't trust that as far as I can throw it.”
–Woodie
“Meddling with it will only cause further trouble.”
–Maxwell
“Tis a gate betwixt realms.”
–Wigfrid
“This isn't home!”
–Webber
“One day I'll find the portal that takes me home...”
–Warly
“Not working”
–Wormwood
“Just like the one I came through...”
–Winona
“A fire-powered hopper.”
–Wortox
“How that door work?”
–Wurt
“That's how we got here. Hopefully how we get back, too.”
–Walter
“That's how we got here. Hopefully how we get back, too.”
–Wanda
In The Forge event, the players spawn in a plume of flames via the Molten Gateway into the Forge Gate Realm. Players must win against the minions of Battlemaster Pugna in order to activate the Gateway with the Ancient Key and leave this Gate Realm. The Molten Gateway is technically a separate structure sharing the same name as the one located in the Atrium. It currently has no other function, but the game files reveal that it also has an active state, which might be used in later versions of the event.
The Gorge[edit | edit source]
Ancient Gateway
Source
The Gorge
Miscellaneous
"quagmire_portal"
Only during the Event: The Gorge
“Another dead end.”
–Wilson
“I'm just gonna keep jumping through portals til I figure out a way home.”
–Willow
“It did not go home. Is no surprise.”
–Wolfgang
“There's no night here. It is a nice change.”
–Wendy
“IT DID NOT FUNCTION AS I HAD HOPED”
–WX-78
“My suspicions were correct. It has more than one destination.”
–Wickerbottom
“Looks like we're going nowhere fast, Lucy.”
–Woodie
“Nobody listens to me.”
–Maxwell
“Tis clösed tö us.”
–Wigfrid
“At least it took us somewhere different.”
–Webber
“Can't go out”
–Wormwood
“I might not be good at rescue missions.”
–Winona
“A way out or in, depending who you are.”
–Wortox
“Another dead end.”
–Wurt
“Another dead end.”
–Walter
“Another dead end.”
–Wanda
In The Gorge event, the players spawn in a gush of water via the Mossy Gateway into the Gorge Gate Realm. Chronologically, this event takes place directly after The Forge, meaning that entering the Molten Gateway took the characters straight to this dimension. Players must cook food to satisfy the Gnaw and gain three of the Gnaw's Favors, which are used to charge the Ancient Key and thus open the Mossy Gateway. The Mossy Gateway is technically a separate structure sharing the same name as the one located in the Atrium.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Ancient Gateway was introduced in the Heart of the Ruins update.
When first released, the Ancient Gateway had black and yellow caution tape all over it, to signalize it was still a work in progress feature. During this time, various Items were able to be placed in, such as the Iridescent Gem. Picking it back out always produced an Ancient Key. When examining an Ancient Gateway with a key inserted, the player character said: "The lights reveal ancient text: Come Back Next Update."
The Gateway is featured in the What Lies Beyond? video clip, for a brief moment revealing a silhouette of Winona before her official reveal.
The Ancient Gateway located in the Ruins area of the Constant is also called the Atrium Gate.
WX-78's examination quote for an inactive Ancient Gateway is "ERROR 502". Error 502 is an error that occurs when a server receives an invalid response from another server due to a "bad gateway", thus being used to refer to the non-working state of this structure.
Ancient Gateway is automatically repaired if Shadow Rifts is set to “Always” in world settings.

It currently has no other function, but the game files reveal that it also has an active state, which might be used in later versions of the event. {{clear}} == {{Pic|32|Silver Bowl}} The Gorge == {{Object Infobox |name = Ancient Gateway |image = Mossy Gateway.png |biome = The Gorge |spawnCode = "quagmire_portal" }} {{Event|The Gorge}} {{Quotes |wilson = Another dead end. |willow = I'm just gonna keep jumping through portals til I figure out a way home. |wolfgang = It did not go home. Is no surprise. |wendy = There's no night here. It is a nice change. |wx78 = IT DID NOT FUNCTION AS I HAD HOPED |wickerbottom = My suspicions were correct. It has more than one destination. |woodie = Looks like we're going nowhere fast, Lucy. |maxwell = Nobody listens to me. |wigfrid = Tis clösed tö us. |webber = At least it took us somewhere different. |winona = I might not be good at rescue missions. |wortox = A way out or in, depending who you are. |wormwood = Can't go out |wurt = Another dead end. |walter = Another dead end. |wanda = Another dead end. |choose = willow }} In ''[[The Gorge]]'' event, the players spawn in a gush of water via the '''Mossy Gateway''' into the Gorge Gate Realm. Chronologically, this event takes place directly after The Forge, meaning that entering the Molten Gateway took the characters straight to this dimension. Players must cook [[The Gorge Recipes|food]] to satisfy the [[Gnaw]] and gain three of the [[Gnaw's Favor]]s, which are used to charge the [[Ancient Key#The Gorge|Ancient Key]] and thus open the Mossy Gateway. The Mossy Gateway is technically a separate structure sharing the same name as the one located in the Atrium. {{clear}} == {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Ancient Gateway was introduced in the ''[[A New Reign#February 23rd, 2017 - Heart of the Ruins (Part 1)|Heart of the Ruins]]'' update. * When first released, the Ancient Gateway had black and yellow caution tape all over it, to signalize it was still a work in progress feature. During this time, various [[Items]] were able to be placed in, such as the [[Iridescent Gem]]. Picking it back out always produced an Ancient Key. When examining an Ancient Gateway with a key inserted, the player character said: "The lights reveal ancient text: Come Back Next Update." * The Gateway is featured in the ''[[Animated Shorts and Trailers#What Lies Beyond?|What Lies Beyond?]]'' video clip, for a brief moment revealing a silhouette of [[Winona]] before her official reveal. * The Ancient Gateway located in the [[Ruins]] area of the [[Constant]] is also called the Atrium Gate. * WX-78's examination quote for an inactive Ancient Gateway is "ERROR 502". Error 502 is an error that occurs when a server receives an invalid response from another server due to a "bad gateway", thus being used to refer to the non-working state of this structure. * Ancient Gateway is automatically repaired if Shadow Rifts is set to “Always” in world settings. == {{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery == 20170325171350_1.jpg|The Gateway exploding. 20170325171747_1.jpg|After the explosions.
